page: Amazon Web Services c8a.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features a dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 processor with 48 physical cores and 48 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 96.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps.

Benchmark results place the c8a.12xlarge in the top tier for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and Geekbench. The AMD EPYC architecture, supported by AVX-512 vector extensions including AVX512_VNNI and AVX512_BF16, enables high-throughput execution for complex mathematical operations, ray tracing, and image processing. Memory performance is robust, with DDR5 memory bandwidth reaching top-tier status in small-block cached reads and large-block writes, while large-block uncached reads perform in the top 25% tier.

The instance demonstrates high efficiency for compute-bound workloads that benefit from high clock speeds and dedicated physical cores without SMT overhead. It achieves top-tier performance in software compilation, database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving. Additionally, it handles LLM inference tasks efficiently, processing smaller 135M models in the top 10% tier and larger 7B or 70B models in the top 25% tier. This profile makes the c8a.12xlarge highly suitable for high-performance computing, batch processing, CI/CD pipelines, and web serving workloads where memory-to-CPU ratios of 2 GB per core are optimal.
description: Amazon Web Services c8a.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 processor. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 48 physical cores and 48 vCPUs at 4.5 GHz, paired with 96.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, but features a 22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in single-core and multi-core CPU execution, software compilation, and database operations, alongside strong memory bandwidth. Supported by AVX-512 instruction sets, the c8a.12xlarge is highly efficient for compute-intensive tasks. It is best suited for workloads such as high-performance computing, batch processing, CI/CD compilation, and web serving where a 2.0 GB per core memory allocation is sufficient.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 48 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 96 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 22 Gbps network baseline, delivering top-tier CPU performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 48 AMD EPYC cores, 96 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier benchmark performance.
tagline: A high-frequency, compute-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high-speed DDR5 memory for intensive processing workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 processor with 48 physical cores running at 4.5 GHz.
96.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory providing 2.0 GB per core.
Top-tier single-core and multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng and Geekbench.
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration.
22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU.
Optimized for software compilation, database operations, and high-throughput web serving.
